title: forsskaolea tenacissima
intro: Forsskaolea tenacissima, also known as the Kermadec or New Zealand kauri tree, is a rare and endangered tree species native to the Kermadec Islands. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to inhibit inflammation and reduce oxidative stress. The bark and leaves of the Forsskaolea tenacissima contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the Forsskaolea tenacissima are typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
